chapter: Chapter 5: Tribes, Nomads and Settled Communities

questions.question: Which of the following statements about tribal society is correct?

options.A: Tribal society had a rigid caste system
options.B: Tribal communities were egalitarian with shared resources
options.C: Tribal communities had no social organization

questions.explanation: Tribal societies were largely egalitarian and shared resources within the community.

questions.question: Why were pastoral nomads constantly on the move?

options.A: To trade manufactured goods
options.B: To find new pastures for their livestock

questions.explanation: Pastoral nomads moved seasonally to ensure their livestock had sufficient grazing land.

questions.question: Settled communities differed from nomadic groups primarily because they:

options.A: Had permanent homes and cultivated land
options.B: Did not hunt or gather food

questions.explanation: Settled communities practiced agriculture and lived permanently in one location.

questions.question: Which of the following tribes is known for combining agriculture with hunting?

options.A: Bhils
options.B: Gonds

questions.explanation: Gonds practiced settled agriculture but also hunted and gathered forest produce.

questions.question: Which of the following factors influenced the settlement of tribes near rivers?

options.A: Availability of fertile land and water
options.B: Presence of government offices

questions.explanation: Rivers provided fertile soil for cultivation and water for daily use, attracting settled communities.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true about tribal crafts?

options.A: Tribal crafts were mainly functional and symbolic
options.B: Tribal crafts were industrially manufactured
options.C: Tribes did not make any craft items
options.D: Tribal crafts were imported from cities

questions.explanation: Tribal crafts were both functional (like tools) and symbolic (used in rituals or decoration).

questions.question: Why is oral tradition important in tribal communities?

options.A: It replaces all forms of writing
options.B: It passes down stories, knowledge, and customs from generation to generation
options.C: It is used to trade goods
options.D: It is only used for counting numbers

questions.explanation: Tribes pass knowledge and cultural values orally, as most did not use written language.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the Bhils is correct?

options.A: They are primarily pastoral nomads
options.B: They are known for archery and forest-based occupations
options.C: They live only in Tamil Nadu

questions.explanation: Bhils are forest dwellers, known for hunting, archery, and related occupations.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the Gonds’ social organization?

options.A: Individualistic and competitive
options.B: Community-based with leaders chosen for wisdom

questions.explanation: Gonds lived in communities with leaders selected for their experience and wisdom.

questions.question: Warli paintings are significant because they:

options.A: Reflect urban industrial life
options.B: Depict tribal daily life and nature

questions.explanation: Warli paintings depict the life, culture, and environment of tribal communities.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of nomadic housing?

options.A: Permanent brick houses
options.B: Temporary shelters made of wood and leaves

questions.explanation: Nomads constructed temporary shelters suited to their mobile lifestyle.

questions.question: Which statement is true about tribal economy?

options.A: Tribes depended largely on trade and commerce
options.B: Tribes had a subsistence economy based on forest produce, hunting, and agriculture
options.D: Tribes depended solely on government aid

questions.explanation: Tribal communities produced mainly for their own needs, using forest produce and agriculture.

questions.question: What was the main purpose of festivals in tribal societies?

options.A: Trade negotiations
options.B: Religious, agricultural, and social purposes

questions.explanation: Festivals reinforced cultural beliefs, celebrated harvests, and strengthened community bonds.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about tribal leadership?

options.A: Leaders were chosen randomly
options.B: Leaders were often elders respected for wisdom and bravery
options.C: Leadership was based on wealth alone
options.D: Leaders were elected through formal elections

questions.explanation: Tribal leaders were usually elders or experienced individuals respected for wisdom.

questions.question: Which of the following shows the relationship between nomads and settled communities?

options.A: Nomads often traded forest produce and livestock products with settled communities
options.B: They never interacted
options.C: Nomads imposed taxes on settled communities
options.D: Settled communities were hostile to nomads always

questions.explanation: Nomads and settled communities interacted mainly through trade and exchange of goods.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about tribal religious practices?

options.A: They had no belief in nature spirits
options.B: They often worshipped natural elements like trees, rivers, and animals

questions.explanation: Tribes often worshipped natural elements and spirits, reflecting their close relationship with nature.

questions.question: Which of the following tribes is mainly found in Tamil Nadu and is known for pastoralism?

options.A: Toda
options.B: Bhil

questions.explanation: Toda tribe of Nilgiris practiced pastoralism and is known for their distinctive culture.

questions.question: What was the role of women in tribal societies?

options.A: They did not participate in economic activities
options.B: They actively participated in agriculture, craft, and gathering
options.C: They were confined to homes only

questions.explanation: Women in tribal societies participated in various economic and cultural activities.

questions.question: Why did some tribes practice shifting cultivation?

options.A: To allow land to regain fertility
options.B: Because they were nomadic

questions.explanation: Shifting cultivation allowed the soil to recover fertility before being used again.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about tribal festivals?

options.A: They had no connection with agriculture
options.B: They marked important agricultural and seasonal events
options.C: They were only for trade purposes

questions.explanation: Tribal festivals celebrated seasonal changes, harvests, and religious beliefs.

questions.question: Which tribe is known for their iron smelting and weapon making in central India?

options.A: Gonds
options.B: Toda

questions.explanation: Gonds in central India were skilled in iron smelting and making weapons.

questions.question: Why were some nomadic tribes considered semi-settled?

options.A: They had permanent homes
options.B: They combined seasonal movement with some permanent agriculture

questions.explanation: Some tribes practiced both seasonal movement and agriculture in certain areas.

questions.question: Which of the following shows the cultural contribution of tribes?

options.A: Traditional art, music, dance, and crafts
options.B: Modern industrial design

questions.explanation: Tribal communities contributed rich cultural heritage through art, music, dance, and crafts.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct about tribal land ownership?

options.A: Land was privately owned
options.B: Land was mostly communally owned
options.C: Tribes did not use land for cultivation
options.D: Land was owned by the government only

questions.explanation: Tribal lands were generally communally owned and used by the whole community.

questions.question: Which of the following best explains why tribal communities preserved forests?

options.A: They worshipped nature and depended on forests for livelihood
options.B: They wanted industrial growth
options.C: They built cities in the forest
options.D: They had no use for forests

questions.explanation: Tribes depended on forests for food, fuel, and cultural reasons, leading to conservation practices.
